definition: Tax Deducted at Source — tax your employer or payer deducts before crediting your income.
example: If your salary is ₹1,00,000/month and TDS is ₹5,000, you receive ₹95,000 in hand.

definition: Income Tax Return — the annual form you file with the Income Tax Department declaring your income, deductions, and tax paid.
example: ITR-1 (Sahaj) is for salaried individuals with income up to ₹50 lakh.

definition: Permanent Account Number — a 10-character alphanumeric identifier issued by the IT Department.
example: ABCDE1234F is a valid PAN format.

definition: Annual Information Statement — a comprehensive document showing all financial transactions reported to the IT Department against your PAN.
example: AIS shows your salary, bank interest, mutual fund transactions, and TDS in one place.

definition: A TDS certificate issued by your employer showing your salary details and the tax deducted.
example: Your employer provides Form 16 by June 15 after each financial year.

definition: Annual tax statement showing all taxes deposited against your PAN.
example: Cross-check Form 26AS with Form 16 to ensure TDS is correctly deposited.

definition: Section 80C allows deductions up to ₹1.5 lakh for investments like PPF, ELSS, NSC, LIC premiums.
example: Invest ₹1.5 lakh in PPF to reduce taxable income by ₹1.5 lakh under 80C.

definition: Section 80D allows deductions for health insurance premiums — up to ₹25,000 for self/family.
example: Pay ₹20,000 for family health insurance to claim ₹20,000 deduction under 80D.

definition: House Rent Allowance — a component of salary that partially or fully exempts rent paid from tax.
example: If you pay ₹15,000/month rent in a metro city, most of it can be exempt under HRA.

definition: A flat ₹50,000 deduction from salary income available to all salaried employees — no proof required.
example: With ₹8,00,000 salary, your income after standard deduction is ₹7,50,000.

definition: Tax regime with higher rates but allows deductions under 80C, 80D, HRA, etc.
example: Old regime benefits those with large 80C investments and HRA claims.

definition: Tax regime with lower slab rates but most deductions are not allowed.
example: New regime is simpler and better for those without major deductions.

definition: Public Provident Fund — a government-backed savings scheme with 15-year lock-in, eligible for 80C deduction.
example: Invest up to ₹1.5 lakh/year in PPF for tax-free returns.

definition: Equity Linked Savings Scheme — mutual funds with 3-year lock-in eligible for 80C deduction.
example: ELSS has the shortest lock-in among 80C instruments and offers market-linked returns.

definition: Amount the IT Department returns to you when TDS paid exceeds actual tax liability.
example: If TDS was ₹85,000 but tax liability is ₹56,700, you get ₹28,300 as refund.
